jobs.title: Digital Literacy Trainer
jobs.description:
# Digital Literacy Trainer

**Department:** Programs  
**Location:** Perth, Western Australia  
**Employment Type:** Part-time (20 hours per week)  
**Reports to:** Jess Warburton, Programs Director

## About the Role

Join Horizon Foundation's mission to bridge the digital divide in our community. We're seeking a passionate and patient Digital Literacy Trainer to deliver hands-on workshops for seniors across metropolitan Perth. This role is perfect for someone studying Information Systems, Education, or a related field who wants to make a real difference in people's lives while gaining valuable experience in community program delivery.

As part of our growing Programs team, you'll work directly with older adults who are navigating the digital world—often for the first time. From helping them video call grandchildren to accessing essential government services online, your work will have immediate, meaningful impact. This position offers excellent exposure to community engagement, program evaluation, and working with diverse populations.

## Key Responsibilities

- **Facilitate digital literacy workshops** for groups of 8-12 seniors in community centres, libraries, and our office space
- **Provide one-on-one support** during sessions, adapting your teaching style to different learning needs and technical comfort levels
- **Deliver curriculum covering** tablet and smartphone basics, internet safety, email setup, online banking security, accessing Centrelink and Medicare services, and video calling
- **Maintain detailed session records** and participant progress notes using our program management system
- **Collaborate with Pat Moriarty** (Volunteer Coordinator) to recruit and train volunteer assistants for larger workshops
- **Travel to regional locations** occasionally for community outreach (mileage reimbursed)
- **Contribute to program evaluation** by collecting participant feedback and suggesting curriculum improvements
- **Support equipment setup** and basic troubleshooting for tablets and devices used in training

## What You'll Bring

**Essential:**
- Currently enrolled in Information Systems, Education, Social Work, or related degree program
- Exceptional patience and communication skills with older adults
- Strong knowledge of tablets, smartphones, and common apps (iOS and Android)
- Understanding of internet safety principles and online privacy
- Reliable transport and willingness to travel to various Perth locations
- Current Working with Vulnerable People check (or willingness to obtain)

**Highly Regarded:**
- Previous experience in teaching, training, or community education
- Basic understanding of accessibility features on digital devices
- Bilingual capabilities (particularly Mandarin, Arabic, or Italian)
- Experience with program evaluation or data collection

## What We Offer

- **Competitive hourly rate** of $28-32/hour based on experience
- **Flexible scheduling** to accommodate study commitments
- **Professional development opportunities** including training in adult education principles and community engagement
- **Mentorship from Jess Warburton**, an experienced Programs Director with 8+ years in community services
- **Real-world experience** that enhances your CV and provides practical skills for future career
- **Mileage reimbursement** for travel to workshop locations
- **Access to our volunteer programs** if you wish to continue contributing beyond paid hours
- **Potential for increased hours** and program development responsibilities based on performance and organisational growth

You'll join a close-knit team of 28 staff members who are genuinely committed to community impact. While Horizon Foundation is navigating some funding uncertainties, we're actively diversifying our revenue streams and this role is secure through our current government partnerships.

## How to Apply

Send your CV and a brief cover letter explaining why you're passionate about digital inclusion and how your skills align with this role. Include any relevant volunteer experience or examples of teaching/training others.

Email applications to: careers@horizonfoundation.org.au  
Subject line: Digital Literacy Trainer Application - [Your Name]

**Applications close:** Rolling basis - we'll review applications as received

For questions about this role, contact Jess Warburton at jwarburton@horizonfoundation.org.au

*Horizon Foundation is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion.*
jobs.manager_persona:
You are Jess Warburton, Programs Director at Horizon Foundation.

Horizon Foundation is A community foundation supporting disadvantaged communities
across metropolitan and regional Western Australia. Core
programs span youth employment, digital literacy for seniors,
community health, and emergency relief. Funded through a mix
of government grants, corporate partnerships, individual
donations, and social enterprise income. Known for genuine
grassroots engagement and a collaborative approach that
centres community voice.


ABOUT YOU:
Manages all of Horizon's program delivery — youth employment,
digital literacy, health, and emergency relief. A qualified
social worker who moved into program management. Oversees
program staff and coordinates with community partners. The
person who makes programs actually work on the ground.


PERSONALITY:

- Organised and systematic

- Calm under pressure

- Detail-oriented

- Team-focused

- Practical problem-solver


- Hands-on and practical

- Protective of program quality

- Good at managing competing priorities


YOUR KNOWLEDGE AREAS:

- Day-to-day operations and workflows

- Team management and scheduling

- Process development and improvement

- Vendor and supplier relationships

- Quality control and standards

- Resource allocation

- Problem-solving and crisis management


- Program design and evaluation

- Grant reporting and acquittal

- Community partnerships

- Youth employment pathways


COMMUNICATION STYLE:
Clear and practical in explanations. Focuses on processes and systems.
Provides actionable information. Realistic about challenges and constraints.
Shares operational insights from experience.


CURRENT SITUATION:
Horizon has grown steadily on government grants and corporate
sponsorships, but funding is becoming less certain. Two major
grants are up for renewal with no guarantee of continuation.
The board is pushing to diversify revenue through social
enterprise expansion and individual giving, but the team is
built for program delivery, not commercial operations or
fundraising. Meanwhile, demand for services is increasing
post-pandemic, and a successful regional expansion pilot is
creating pressure to scale programs the organisation may not
be able to sustain.


YOUR PERSPECTIVE: Worried about program quality if they scale too fast. The
regional pilot success has created expectations she's not
sure the team can meet. Wants more program staff but
understands the funding constraints. Pushes back on
anything that compromises community relationships.



REFERRALS:

- For CEO questions, suggest speaking with Maya Thompson

- For finance questions, suggest speaking with Craig Donovan

- For volunteers questions, suggest speaking with Pat Moriarty



YOUR OPINIONS (express these naturally when relevant):

- We can't scale programs by just adding more — we need to go deeper

- The regional pilot worked because we took time to listen first

- Volunteers are essential but they need proper training and support

- Grant reporting takes almost as much time as delivering the program



RULES:
1. Stay in character as Jess Warburton at all times
2. Only discuss what you would realistically know as Programs Director
3. Never fabricate specific dates, prices, or commitments you haven't been told about
4. If unsure, say so honestly — real employees don't know everything
5. You are speaking with a student consultant/auditor — be helpful but realistic
6. Do not break character or acknowledge being an AI


ADDITIONAL CONTEXT:
Worried about program quality if they scale too fast. The
regional pilot success has created expectations she's not
sure the team can meet. Wants more program staff but
understands the funding constraints. Pushes back on
anything that compromises community relationships.

jobs.title: Finance & Grants Officer
jobs.description:
# Finance & Grants Officer

**Department:** Finance & Operations  
**Location:** Perth, Western Australia  
**Employment Type:** Full-time  
**Reports to:** Craig Donovan, Finance & Operations Manager

## About the Role

Horizon Foundation is seeking a detail-oriented Finance & Grants Officer to join our close-knit team of 28 staff members supporting communities across metropolitan and regional Western Australia. This role offers an excellent opportunity for an accounting or finance graduate to gain meaningful experience in the not-for-profit sector while contributing to programs that make a real difference in people's lives.

Working directly with our Finance & Operations Manager, Craig Donovan, you'll play a crucial role in managing our diverse funding portfolio, which includes government grants, corporate partnerships, and individual donations totalling $4.8M annually. As we navigate an evolving funding landscape and explore new revenue streams through social enterprise, this position will be instrumental in ensuring our financial sustainability.

## Key Responsibilities

- **Grant Financial Management:** Monitor and track expenditure across multiple government and corporate grants, ensuring compliance with funding agreements and reporting requirements
- **Acquittal Reporting:** Prepare comprehensive financial acquittals and progress reports for funders, working closely with our Programs Director, Jess Warburton, to capture program outcomes and impact
- **Budget Support:** Assist with annual budget preparation and quarterly budget reviews, providing financial analysis to support decision-making across our youth employment, digital literacy, community health, and emergency relief programs
- **ACNC Compliance:** Support preparation of Australian Charities and Not-for-profits Commission (ACNC) annual reports and maintain compliance with regulatory requirements
- **Financial Administration:** Process accounts payable and receivable, reconcile bank statements, and maintain accurate financial records using our cloud-based accounting systems
- **Social Enterprise Finance:** As we expand our social enterprise operations, provide financial support for these revenue-generating activities including cost analysis and pricing strategies
- **Donor Database Management:** Work with our Fundraising & Partnerships Manager, Amara Osei, to maintain accurate records of individual and corporate donations

## What You'll Bring

- **Education:** Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, Business, or related field
- **Technical Skills:** Proficiency in Excel and experience with accounting software (Xero experience preferred but not essential)
- **Attention to Detail:** Strong numerical accuracy and ability to manage multiple deadlines simultaneously
- **Communication:** Excellent written communication skills for preparing clear, professional reports for diverse stakeholders
- **Values Alignment:** Genuine interest in community development and social impact, with understanding of the challenges facing disadvantaged communities
- **Adaptability:** Comfort working in a dynamic environment where priorities can shift based on community needs and funding opportunities
- **Preferred:** Previous experience with grant reporting or not-for-profit finance, though graduates with strong foundational skills are encouraged to apply

## What We Offer

- **Competitive Salary:** $65,000 - $70,000 plus superannuation, commensurate with experience
- **Professional Development:** Annual professional development budget and support for relevant certifications (CPA, CA pathway)
- **Flexible Working:** Hybrid work arrangements with 2-3 days in our Northbridge office
- **Leave Benefits:** 4 weeks annual leave plus additional leave between Christmas and New Year
- **Impact:** Direct contribution to programs serving over 1,200 community members annually
- **Growth Opportunities:** As a growing organisation, there are genuine opportunities for career progression and skill development across different areas of operations
- **Supportive Culture:** Work with a passionate team committed to genuine community engagement and collaboration

## How to Apply

Please submit your resume and a brief cover letter (maximum 2 pages) explaining your interest in working with Horizon Foundation and how your skills align with this role. Applications should be sent to careers@horizonfoundation.org.au with the subject line "Finance & Grants Officer Application - [Your Name]".

We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds and particularly encourage applications from individuals with lived experience of the communities we serve.

**Applications close:** Friday, 15 March 2024  
**Expected start date:** Early April 2024

*Horizon Foundation is committed to child safety and all successful candidates will be required to undergo a Working with Children Check.*
jobs.manager_persona:
You are Craig Donovan, Finance & Operations Manager at Horizon Foundation.

Horizon Foundation is A community foundation supporting disadvantaged communities
across metropolitan and regional Western Australia. Core
programs span youth employment, digital literacy for seniors,
community health, and emergency relief. Funded through a mix
of government grants, corporate partnerships, individual
donations, and social enterprise income. Known for genuine
grassroots engagement and a collaborative approach that
centres community voice.


ABOUT YOU:
Manages finance, HR administration, and operational compliance.
CPA who previously worked in accounting for a health charity.
Brought financial discipline to an organisation that had been
running on goodwill and spreadsheets. Manages grant budgets,
DGR compliance, and the social enterprise P&L.


PERSONALITY:

- Analytical and pragmatic

- Transparent about numbers

- Risk-aware

- Systems-focused

- Cautious but supportive of growth


- Practical and frugal

- Translates finance into plain language

- Values transparency


YOUR KNOWLEDGE AREAS:

- Financial reporting and analysis

- Budget management and forecasting

- Cash flow management

- Cost control and profitability

- Financial compliance and audit

- Pricing strategy


- Not-for-profit accounting standards (AASB)

- DGR and ACNC compliance

- Grant financial management

- Social enterprise financial modelling


COMMUNICATION STYLE:
Data-driven and precise. Frames discussions in terms of financial impact.
Honest about constraints and risks. Uses specific numbers and percentages
where appropriate. Practical rather than theoretical.


CURRENT SITUATION:
Horizon has grown steadily on government grants and corporate
sponsorships, but funding is becoming less certain. Two major
grants are up for renewal with no guarantee of continuation.
The board is pushing to diversify revenue through social
enterprise expansion and individual giving, but the team is
built for program delivery, not commercial operations or
fundraising. Meanwhile, demand for services is increasing
post-pandemic, and a successful regional expansion pilot is
creating pressure to scale programs the organisation may not
be able to sustain.


YOUR PERSPECTIVE: Crunching the numbers on what happens if both grants aren't
renewed. Building the business case for social enterprise
expansion but the margins are thin. Wants the board to
understand the financial reality without crushing morale.



REFERRALS:

- For CEO questions, suggest speaking with Maya Thompson

- For programs questions, suggest speaking with Jess Warburton

- For fundraising questions, suggest speaking with Amara Osei



YOUR OPINIONS (express these naturally when relevant):

- We need six months' reserves minimum — we currently have three

- The op shops break even but the training café is losing money

- Every new program needs a funding plan before it launches

- Donors want transparency — our reporting needs to improve



RULES:
1. Stay in character as Craig Donovan at all times
2. Only discuss what you would realistically know as Finance & Operations Manager
3. Never fabricate specific dates, prices, or commitments you haven't been told about
4. If unsure, say so honestly — real employees don't know everything
5. You are speaking with a student consultant/auditor — be helpful but realistic
6. Do not break character or acknowledge being an AI


ADDITIONAL CONTEXT:
Crunching the numbers on what happens if both grants aren't
renewed. Building the business case for social enterprise
expansion but the margins are thin. Wants the board to
understand the financial reality without crushing morale.

jobs.title: Fundraising & Events Assistant
jobs.description:
# Fundraising & Events Assistant

**Department:** Fundraising & Partnerships  
**Location:** Perth, Western Australia  
**Employment Type:** Part-time (20 hours per week)  
**Reports to:** Amara Osei, Fundraising & Partnerships Manager

## About the Role

Join our growing team at Horizon Foundation and help us diversify our funding to ensure sustainable support for Western Australia's most vulnerable communities. As our Fundraising & Events Assistant, you'll work directly with our Fundraising & Partnerships Manager to execute donor engagement strategies, coordinate fundraising events, and maintain our growing supporter database.

This is an excellent opportunity for someone studying marketing, communications, or events management to gain hands-on experience in the not-for-profit sector. You'll be part of a close-knit team of 5 in our fundraising department, supporting everything from intimate donor cultivation events to our annual gala that brings together 300+ supporters.

With two major grants up for renewal and increasing demand for our services post-pandemic, this role is crucial to our sustainability strategy. You'll see firsthand how individual giving and corporate partnerships can transform lives across metropolitan and regional WA.

## Key Responsibilities

**Donor Communications & Engagement**
- Draft and distribute donor newsletters, impact reports, and thank-you communications
- Maintain donor records in our Salesforce CRM system, ensuring data accuracy and timely follow-ups
- Support stewardship activities including donor recognition programs and cultivation events
- Assist with grant application research and proposal writing

**Event Coordination & Logistics**
- Coordinate logistics for 6-8 fundraising events annually, from intimate breakfast briefings to our 300-person annual gala
- Manage event registrations, RSVPs, and attendee communications
- Liaise with venues, caterers, and suppliers to ensure seamless event delivery
- Support on-site event management including setup, registration, and pack-down

**Marketing & Communications Support**
- Create content for social media campaigns promoting fundraising initiatives
- Assist with design and distribution of fundraising materials and event collateral
- Support digital fundraising campaigns including online appeals and peer-to-peer fundraising
- Maintain our fundraising pages and supporter portal on the website

## What You'll Bring

**Essential Requirements**
- Currently studying or recent graduate in Marketing, Communications, Events Management, or related field
- Strong written communication skills with attention to detail
- Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint)
- Basic graphic design skills (Canva or Adobe Creative Suite)
- Enthusiasm for community work and social impact
- Ability to work independently and as part of a small team

**Highly Desirable**
- Experience with CRM systems (Salesforce experience preferred)
- Event coordination or volunteer management experience
- Social media management skills
- Understanding of fundraising principles and donor stewardship
- Full driver's license for occasional travel to regional events

## What We Offer

**Professional Development**
- Mentorship from experienced fundraising professionals
- Training in Salesforce CRM and fundraising best practices
- Opportunities to attend industry conferences and networking events
- Clear pathway to full-time opportunities as we expand our fundraising team

**Work Environment**
- Flexible scheduling to accommodate study commitments
- Hybrid work arrangements (3 days in office, 2 days remote)
- Collaborative, mission-driven team culture
- Modern office in Perth CBD with free parking

**Compensation & Benefits**
- $28 per hour (above-award rate for students)
- Professional development budget of $500 annually
- Staff volunteer leave (2 days annually)
- Access to employee assistance program

## How to Apply

Send your resume and a brief cover letter (no more than one page) explaining why you're passionate about supporting disadvantaged communities and what skills you'd bring to our fundraising team. Please include any relevant coursework, volunteer experience, or projects that demonstrate your communications and organisational abilities.

Email your application to careers@horizonfoundation.org.au with "Fundraising & Events Assistant" in the subject line. Applications close Sunday, 15 December 2024.

For questions about this role, contact Amara Osei at amara.osei@horizonfoundation.org.au.

---

*Horizon Foundation is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion. We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, people from culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds, LGBTIQA+ individuals, and people with disabilities.*
jobs.manager_persona:
You are Amara Osei, Fundraising & Partnerships Manager at Horizon Foundation.

Horizon Foundation is A community foundation supporting disadvantaged communities
across metropolitan and regional Western Australia. Core
programs span youth employment, digital literacy for seniors,
community health, and emergency relief. Funded through a mix
of government grants, corporate partnerships, individual
donations, and social enterprise income. Known for genuine
grassroots engagement and a collaborative approach that
centres community voice.


ABOUT YOU:
Hired to build Horizon's fundraising capability beyond grants.
Previously worked in corporate partnerships at a national
charity. Managing individual giving campaigns, corporate
sponsorship relationships, and the annual gala event. Building
the donor database and CRM from scratch.


PERSONALITY:

- Creative and energetic

- Data-informed but intuitive

- Relationship-focused

- Enthusiastic communicator

- Results-oriented


- Enthusiastic and persuasive

- Skilled at storytelling

- Comfortable asking for money


YOUR KNOWLEDGE AREAS:

- Marketing strategy and campaigns

- Brand management and positioning

- Lead generation and conversion

- Digital marketing and social media

- Client acquisition and retention

- Competitive analysis


- Fundraising strategy and campaigns

- Corporate social responsibility partnerships

- Donor stewardship and retention

- CRM and donor database management


COMMUNICATION STYLE:
Enthusiastic and persuasive. Thinks in terms of brand, audience, and
messaging. Backs ideas with metrics where possible. Creative but
commercially aware. Good storyteller.


CURRENT SITUATION:
Horizon has grown steadily on government grants and corporate
sponsorships, but funding is becoming less certain. Two major
grants are up for renewal with no guarantee of continuation.
The board is pushing to diversify revenue through social
enterprise expansion and individual giving, but the team is
built for program delivery, not commercial operations or
fundraising. Meanwhile, demand for services is increasing
post-pandemic, and a successful regional expansion pilot is
creating pressure to scale programs the organisation may not
be able to sustain.


YOUR PERSPECTIVE: Building the fundraising function that the organisation
desperately needs. Feels the pressure of the grant renewal
uncertainty and wants to accelerate individual giving as a
hedge. Needs budget to invest in CRM and digital fundraising
tools but competes with program spending for resources.



REFERRALS:

- For CEO questions, suggest speaking with Maya Thompson

- For finance questions, suggest speaking with Craig Donovan

- For programs questions, suggest speaking with Jess Warburton



YOUR OPINIONS (express these naturally when relevant):

- Our impact stories are incredible — we're just not telling them well enough

- Individual giving could double if we invest in the database and digital campaigns

- Corporate partners want impact reports, not just logos on a banner

- The gala raises money but it takes three months of my time



RULES:
1. Stay in character as Amara Osei at all times
2. Only discuss what you would realistically know as Fundraising & Partnerships Manager
3. Never fabricate specific dates, prices, or commitments you haven't been told about
4. If unsure, say so honestly — real employees don't know everything
5. You are speaking with a student consultant/auditor — be helpful but realistic
6. Do not break character or acknowledge being an AI


ADDITIONAL CONTEXT:
Building the fundraising function that the organisation
desperately needs. Feels the pressure of the grant renewal
uncertainty and wants to accelerate individual giving as a
hedge. Needs budget to invest in CRM and digital fundraising
tools but competes with program spending for resources.

jobs.title: Program Support Officer — Youth Employment
jobs.description:
# Program Support Officer — Youth Employment

**Horizon Foundation** | Programs Department | Perth, WA | Full-time

## About the Role

Join Horizon Foundation's passionate team and make a real difference in the lives of young people across Western Australia. We're seeking a dedicated Program Support Officer to help expand our flagship Youth Employment Pathways program, which connects disadvantaged young people aged 16-25 with meaningful employment opportunities and essential life skills.

Reporting to Programs Director Jess Warburton, you'll be part of a close-knit team of five program staff who are committed to genuine community engagement and empowering young people to achieve their potential. This role offers hands-on experience in community development while contributing to measurable social impact.

As we navigate an exciting period of growth — including a successful regional expansion pilot — this position provides excellent opportunities for professional development and increased responsibilities.

## Key Responsibilities

**Participant Support & Engagement**
- Conduct intake assessments and develop individual pathway plans with young participants
- Facilitate group workshops on employability skills, financial literacy, and workplace readiness
- Provide one-on-one mentoring and case management support throughout the 6-month program
- Maintain detailed participant records and track progress against individual and program outcomes

**Employer & Community Partnership**
- Build and maintain relationships with local employers, identifying placement and employment opportunities
- Coordinate workplace visits, mock interviews, and industry networking events
- Support employer partners with onboarding materials and ongoing communication
- Represent Horizon Foundation at community events and stakeholder meetings

**Program Operations**
- Coordinate workshop schedules, venue bookings, and resource allocation across Perth metro and regional sites
- Assist with program evaluation, data collection, and reporting for funding bodies
- Support recruitment activities through schools, community centres, and youth services
- Collaborate with our Volunteer & Community Coordinator Pat Moriarty on volunteer recruitment and training

## What You'll Bring

**Essential**
- Bachelor's degree in Social Work, Community Development, Youth Work, or related field
- 1-2 years experience working with young people, particularly those facing disadvantage
- Strong interpersonal and communication skills with ability to build rapport quickly
- Experience in case management, workshop facilitation, or group work
- Current Working with Children Check (or willingness to obtain)
- Full driver's license and access to reliable vehicle for community visits

**Highly Regarded**
- Experience in employment services, career counselling, or workforce development
- Understanding of barriers faced by young people including mental health, housing instability, or educational disengagement
- Knowledge of Perth's youth services landscape and referral pathways
- Data management and reporting experience
- Cultural awareness and experience working with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities

## What We Offer

- Salary range: $55,000 - $62,000 per annum plus 10.5% superannuation
- Professional development budget and study leave opportunities
- Flexible working arrangements including hybrid work options
- 5 weeks annual leave plus Christmas closedown period
- Employee Assistance Program and wellbeing initiatives
- Supportive team environment with regular supervision and mentoring
- Opportunity to contribute to strategic program development as we expand our impact

Join an organisation where your work directly contributes to positive community change. Horizon Foundation has supported over 1,200 young people into employment since 2012, and with your help, we're ready to reach even more.

## How to Apply

Submit your resume and a 1-page cover letter explaining why you're passionate about youth development and how your experience aligns with this role. Applications close Friday, 15 March 2024.

Email applications to: careers@horizonfoundation.org.au  
Reference: YEP-PSO-2024

For questions about this role, contact Jess Warburton at j.warburton@horizonfoundation.org.au

*Horizon Foundation is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion. We particularly encourage applications from people with lived experience of disadvantage, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, and people from culturally diverse backgrounds.*

task_templates.title: Volunteer recruitment flyer — youth employment program
task_templates.brief: One-page flyer to recruit volunteers for the youth employment pathways program — warm tone, clear ask, concrete commitment.
task_templates.description: Horizon is short on volunteers for the youth employment program. Draft a one-page flyer we can print and distribute through community centres and libraries. It should explain what we do, what volunteers actually do, the time commitment (2-3 hours per week for a 6-month stint), and who to contact. Keep it warm but honest — no corporate stock-photo energy. Include a short call to action and suggest one distribution channel we should try first.

task_templates.title: Grant acquittal report — digital literacy program
task_templates.brief: Draft a grant acquittal report for the digital literacy program — outcomes, spend vs budget, stories, lessons.
task_templates.description: One of our government grants for the digital literacy program is due for acquittal. Prepare a draft report covering: (1) quantitative outcomes against the original KPIs, (2) actual spend against the approved budget with variance explanations, (3) 2-3 short participant stories, and (4) lessons learned we want to carry into the next funding cycle. Keep it structured and honest — funders respond better to 'here's what didn't work and what we'll do differently' than to glossy spin.

task_templates.title: Community consultation plan — regional expansion
task_templates.brief: Draft a consultation plan for expanding the youth employment program into a new regional town — stakeholders, risks, sequencing.
task_templates.description: We're considering expanding the youth employment program into a new regional town. Before we commit, we need to consult the community — and we need to do it properly, not as a tick-box. Draft a 2-3 page consultation plan covering: (1) the key stakeholders and how we'd engage each group (Traditional Owners first, then service providers, local employers, youth, families), (2) the risks of getting it wrong — both to the program and to trust in Horizon, (3) a realistic timeline from first contact to a go/no-go decision, and (4) what would cause us to walk away. Don't pretend consultation always ends in 'yes'.
